title: I/O error during execution of test_podman_build.sh
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

/**kind bug**


**Description**

While running the test case, https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_build.sh I am frequently seeing errors highlighted below. I have noticed this on `x64` as well as `ppc64le`. Although created images work fine, but nevertheless, during the image creation it throws that error repeatedly. 

  TARGET=volume-image
  podman build -t $TARGET $HOME/test/build/preserve-volumes
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:4fe2ade4980c2dda4fc95858ebb981489baec8c1e4bd282ab1c3560be8ff9bde
 2.10 MB / 2.10 MB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:196d12cf6ab19273823e700516e98eb1910b03b17840f9d5509f03858484d321
 1.48 KB / 1.48 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /vol/subvol/subsubvol
--> c114e6f7465ac6e153b1695b2db9ee2912a565825220f5aa5709a34494e85464
STEP 3: FROM c114e6f7465ac6e153b1695b2db9ee2912a565825220f5aa5709a34494e85464
STEP 4: RUN dd if=/dev/zero bs=512 count=1 of=/vol/subvol/subsubvol/subsubvolfile
**ERRO[0000] read container terminal output: input/output error: input/output error** 
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
--> 7c122e3430b361d297a5f936966a0e0ab8025e8791bcb02385311b679e8ba997
STEP 5: FROM 7c122e3430b361d297a5f936966a0e0ab8025e8791bcb02385311b679e8ba997
STEP 6: VOLUME /vol/subvol
--> 074f5a5f2e85223568ad7475ee40ada461966151a0c85ae204182fee1e18082b
STEP 7: FROM 074f5a5f2e85223568ad7475ee40ada461966151a0c85ae204182fee1e18082b
STEP 8: RUN dd if=/dev/zero bs=512 count=1 of=/vol/subvol/subvolfile
**ERRO[0000] read container terminal output: input/output error: input/output error** 
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
--> b664e0a492bf6acc78d0855f8a00a1b9d027ec6c165128887602efea30a14ac3
STEP 9: FROM b664e0a492bf6acc78d0855f8a00a1b9d027ec6c165128887602efea30a14ac3
STEP 10: RUN dd if=/dev/zero bs=512 count=1 of=/vol/volfile
**ERRO[0000] read container terminal output: input/output error: input/output error** 
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
--> 85f88e63c3f448a9bd7e2658a324cf3de040f8d920ddca87aac491586642cdf6
STEP 11: FROM 85f88e63c3f448a9bd7e2658a324cf3de040f8d920ddca87aac491586642cdf6
STEP 12: VOLUME /vol
--> 265fe1becb814f2ef28afd5060d8df04c7258f9935f54878a4f263589673f71c
STEP 13: FROM 265fe1becb814f2ef28afd5060d8df04c7258f9935f54878a4f263589673f71c
STEP 14: VOLUME /vol/subvol
--> 2f523d1ede23f4b00ba4e4f690f0481ecee343a12ae9a5dc41c46df47eefddb9
STEP 15: FROM 2f523d1ede23f4b00ba4e4f690f0481ecee343a12ae9a5dc41c46df47eefddb9
STEP 16: RUN dd if=/dev/zero bs=512 count=1 of=/vol/anothervolfile
**ERRO[0000] read container terminal output: input/output error: input/output error** 
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
--> 399afb81a6786ed8b141fe36edad7a0dd0c3325f1814ce9bfe6a90ead79a49eb
STEP 17: FROM 399afb81a6786ed8b141fe36edad7a0dd0c3325f1814ce9bfe6a90ead79a49eb
STEP 18: ADD Dockerfile /vol/Dockerfile
--> 24695acd2a4b63c3e62932442c03f12442245ec8e69042108bc5ff1d5f1ffbb5
STEP 19: FROM 24695acd2a4b63c3e62932442c03f12442245ec8e69042108bc5ff1d5f1ffbb5
STEP 20: RUN stat /vol/Dockerfile
**ERRO[0000] read container terminal output: input/output error: input/output error** 
  File: /vol/Dockerfile
  Size: 818       	Blocks: 8          IO Block: 4096   regular file
Device: fd00h/64768d	Inode: 1060693     Links: 1
Access: (0664/-rw-rw-r--)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2018-12-12 08:28:20.000000000
Modify: 2018-12-12 08:28:20.000000000
Change: 2018-12-12 08:49:52.000000000



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Just run https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_build.sh 

**Describe the results you received:**
No errors

**Describe the results you expected:**
I/O error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.10.1.3
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/ppc64le
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.10.1.3-5.gitdb08685.el8+2131+7e3e9e07.ppc64le
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 6a63dada254d8efb3e9b30ed93ad55ab6622bd29-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.0"
  MemFree: 2873229312
  MemTotal: 3722903552
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-52.rc5.dev.git2abd837.el8+1884+9fee228c.ppc64le
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 4382982144
  SwapTotal: 4395565056
  arch: ppc64le
  cpus: 1
  hostname: rhel8
  kernel: 4.18.0-40.el8.ppc64le
  os: linux
  uptime: 50h 47m 42.51s (Approximately 2.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.redhat.io
  - quay.io
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 18
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Observed on both `x64` and `ppc64le`

comments.body: Thanks for the issue report.  I've seen this happen recently in my environment too, but thought it was a local problem on my system.  I think there may be a similar problem reported against Buildah, but I can't find it quickly.  When I've run into it, the issue does not seem to make the command fail, but it sure is ugly.

@harche Could you just make sure this is not an SELinux issue, can you run your test with SELinux in permissive mode.
setenforce 0

@rhatdan I ran the test cases again in SELinux permissive mode, but if failed with the same error. 


[root@rhel8 libpod]# /bin/bash  test/test_podman_build.sh

[root@rhel8 libpod]# 
[root@rhel8 libpod]# 
[root@rhel8 libpod]# getenforce 
Permissive
[root@rhel8 libpod]#
Well that elmiinates SELinux as the culprit.

When this popped up in my environment a while ago, it looked to be some kind of stdio/tty overflow condition.  As I checked with others and they weren't seeing it, I wrote it off to my flakey PC.  I'll try diving back in, but happy to here others thoughts.
@harche @TomSweeneyRedHat Do we still have this issue?

Actually we can close this.  We'd a few changes to how we were handling output  to stderr in the past several loops and I've not seen this happening anywhere since late January when it was a daily occurrence prior.  Wish I'd a PR to point at, but beggars can't be choosers.
